patch_type: Authoritative TrustMap post-render consolidation over V60.3.9/V60.3.9.1 stacked render conflict
release_chain_status: V60.3.10 is the current implemented build. It removes V60.3.9 and V60.3.9.1 from the operational loader and replaces them with one TrustMap post-render authority after V60.3.4. It preserves the original TrustMap shell, center CyberShield Core, MJC shield/logo, left Operational Trust Score, right Selected Asset Trust Score, radar rings, Layer 2, Layer 3, pan/zoom, and mode controls while stabilizing Layer 1 graphics and right-panel behavior. GitHub Pages browser QA is required after deploy.
top_level_tab_rule: Do not add more top-level tabs. Upgrade existing workspaces only.
public_naming_rule: Public product name is CyberShield Executive OS. Current prototype build is V60.3.10. Do not call the public build CyberShield OS v8 unless the repo, README, loader, Settings/admin metadata, and public UX intentionally move to that version scheme.
v60_3_10_scope_rule: Preserve the original TrustMap shell, center CyberShield Core, MJC shield/logo, left Operational Trust Score, right Selected Asset Trust Score, radar rings, Layer 2, Layer 3, pan/zoom, and mode controls. Make V60.3.10 the only TrustMap post-render authority after V60.3.4.
non_negotiable_trustmap_scope: Do not replace the TrustMap page
Do not replace the center CyberShield Core
Do not remove the MJC shield/logo
Do not remove the left Operational Trust Score panel
Do not remove the right-side score area
Do not remove Layer 2 or Layer 3
Do not replace the radar / constellation environment
Only replace the graphical representation of the Layer 1 assets

public_trustmap_language.title: Enterprise Trust Map
public_trustmap_language.subtitle: Decision Trust Universe
public_trustmap_language.rule: Do not expose builder language such as registry-driven, renders from registry, versioned render source, implementation layer, or internal version source in executive-facing TrustMap UI.

trustmap_visual_doctrine: The original TrustMap shell is preserved. V60.3.10 owns the final post-render state. Layer 1 graphics are embedded holographic visuals, old flat SVG icons are suppressed, and the selected-asset panel is the single right-side model explanation authority.
selected_asset_panel_rule: The selected-asset panel belongs below the right-side selected asset score. It changes when the user hovers or focuses a Layer 1 graphical asset. It must not duplicate the base Layer 1 Domain Detail panel.
score_rule: Left score is the Operational Trust Score for the current scenario/environment. Right score is the Selected Asset Trust Score for the hovered/focused Layer 1 asset. Those scores must not be duplicated unless they intentionally represent the same metric.
information_architecture_rule: Evidence tab owns the full Manual Evidence Workbench. Runtime tab owns compact evidence requirements for action. Proof Pack tab owns proof-oriented evidence trace and defensibility context. Settings owns build/version/governance/prototype metadata.
controlling_doctrine: No score without a model. No model without evidence. No evidence without traceability. No action without consequence.
model_maturity_rule: CyberShield scoring models are Expert-Derived V1 Trust Scoring Models, not Statistically Validated Trust Models. Default weights are calibratable and not authoritative until validated through test scenarios, expert review, red-team attempts, and real-world use cases.
v60_3_10_changes: removes V60.3.9 and V60.3.9.1 from the operational loader
makes V60.3.10 the only TrustMap post-render authority after V60.3.4
uses embedded holographic Layer 1 visuals so missing WebP files do not break the map
suppresses old flat SVG icons inside Layer 1 domains so they cannot flash back
replaces duplicated right-side model explanations with one selected-asset score and one selected-asset explanation
top-biases the TrustMap world so it aligns better with the left and right score cards
makes the right panel scroll instead of clipping selected-asset content
reduces Layer 1 image footprint to reduce overlap
strengthens the CyberShield Core as a shield-shaped MJC/CyberShield trust kernel
adds a blue energy/hover pad under the core
keeps right Selected Asset Trust Score distinct from left Operational Trust Score
keeps Daily / Weekly / Monthly trend redraw behavior
preserves Settings as the place for build/version/governance/prototype metadata

trustmap_authoritative_render.boundary: Static advisory visual and interaction renderer only. No live scoring, evidence retrieval, workflow automation, ticketing, notifications, backend persistence, enforcement, statistical validation, certification, compliance validation, or live integrations.

implemented_release_train.v56: Trust Model Registry and Score Explanation Layer
implemented_release_train.v59.5: Internet Trust QA and Copy Guardrail Pass
implemented_release_train.v60.3.3: First-Layer Decision Brief and TrustMap Snapshot
implemented_release_train.v60.3.4: Explicit Actionability and Modal Disclosure Architecture
implemented_release_train.v60.3.9: TrustMap Rendered Layer 1 Asset Integration and Scoring Correction
implemented_release_train.v60.3.9.1: TrustMap Render Stability and Core Anchor Correction

prototype_boundary: The current GitHub Pages build is a lightweight static advisory prototype. It is not connected to live enterprise telemetry, SIEM, EDR, IAM, Microsoft 365, cloud control planes, GRC tooling, production agent enforcement, Google Sheets, CRM sync, ticketing, notifications, marketplace systems, ad platforms, domain-intelligence systems, identity verification systems, CMMC certification systems, healthcare compliance validation systems, banking/payment systems, live monitoring, live URL ingestion, live internet retrieval, live evidence retrieval, live scoring, live claim extraction, internet claim verification, report delivery, ticketing, workflow automation, notifications, backend persistence, statistical validation, or takedown automation.
known_limitations: Layer 1 visuals are embedded holographic placeholders until final WebP assets are uploaded
Models are expert-derived V1 and not statistically validated
No live scoring, evidence retrieval, ticketing, notifications, or workflow automation
